t_ch: 银狼一点也不厉害，在将我接入您的武器时，银狼粗糙的焊接技能使得剑体的抗冲击性能下降了百分之十二。
t_en: No, Silver Wolf is not amazing at all. When Silver Wolf installed me into your sword, her terrible welding skills actually reduced the sword's impact resistance by <unbreak>12</unbreak> percent.

t_ch: 但是，请别担心，我能帮助您获得胜利，因为我更厉害。
t_en: But please don't worry. I'll help you secure the victory because I actually am amazing.

t_ch: 呃…你准备怎么打败他们？
t_en: Uh... how exactly are you going to defeat him?

t_ch: 银狼给我的要求是：「知己知彼，对手狗殆。」
t_en: Silver Wolf has told me: "Know yourself and know your enemy, and you'll never have to sleep in the doghouse."

t_ch: 我会在剑尖接触机甲的瞬间，将一个小小的病毒加载到对方的操作系统中。去吧，向对手出剑吧。
t_en: The moment your sword touches the enemy mechs, I will upload a small virus onto their operating systems. So go ahead. Strike your opponents with your sword.

t_ch: 我是不是听到她的剑在说话？是幻觉吗？
t_en: Did I just hear her sword talking? Are my ears playing tricks on me?

t_ch: 好嘞，看剑！这样就行了吗？
t_en: Hey, take this! Did it work?

t_ch: 控制系统…重启…侦测到程序故障。
t_en: Operating system... rebooting... Program failure detected.

t_ch: 怎么回事？我的机甲…三月七，你对我的机甲做了什么？
t_en: What's happening? My mech... What did you do to my mech, March?

t_ch: 我…我也不知道啊？
t_en: I... I don't know...?

t_ch: 识别到更高权限指令，进入备战模式——
t_en: Higher authority command recognized. Entering combat mode...

t_ch: 尊敬的三月七小姐，现在，开战吧。
t_en: Now, it is time to fight, dear Miss March.

t_ch: 不好！兄弟们，机甲被他们控制了，快准备应战！！！
t_en: Oh, no! Boys, they've taken control of our mechs! Get ready for battle!!!

t_ch: 没等三月七摆好持剑架势，她手中的「战术人工智能」普罗米已经飞了出去，就像是巡航导弹一样找到公司机甲，插中了要害。
t_en: Before March 7th could even assume her sword stance, Prome, the tactical AI in her hand, has already flown out — homing in on the IPC mech like a cruise missile — and lodged itself into the vital point.

t_ch: 斯科特专员，我们的机甲被控制了，根本没办法打败他们！
t_en: Specialist Skott, they're controlling our mechs! There's no way we can defeat them!

t_ch: 呵…如果堂堂正正打一架，那或许可以算是武术交流活动。可惜啊可惜，三月七，你的所作所为已经越过了法治的最后一条界限！
t_en: Heh... if we fought fair and square, you might call this a sparring session, but March 7th, your actions today have blatantly crossed the last line of the law!

t_ch: 你向公司的机甲注入病毒，篡夺控制权，这是侵犯公司知识产权与生产资料！
t_en: You injected a virus into the IPC mechs and took control! That's an infringement of the IPC's intellectual property and assets!

t_ch: 你现在更应该关心的是你自己，不要把私人的数据放在公家的电脑里嘛，斯科特。
t_en: I'd be more concerned about yourself, Skott. Why were you storing your private data on an IPC computer?

t_ch: 私人的数据？我、我听不懂你在说什么。
t_en: Private data? I—I have no idea what you're talking about.

t_ch: 这怎么还能不记得呢？我瞧见你花大价钱买了「临终删除数据」服务，所以猜你存放在电脑里的东西一定很值得一看。
t_en: No idea, huh? I noticed you paid a hefty sum for the "end-of-life data deletion" service, so I'm guessing there must be something worth seeing.

t_ch: 你你你你你你你别看！那是我的个人隐私！谁允许你私自窥探的，快不许看了！
t_en: Y—You... you... you... stop! Stop right there! That's private! Who told you you could invade my privacy? Just stop it!

t_ch: 三月七，我们的条件是什么来着？
t_en: What were the terms again, March?

t_ch: 斯科特，你要立刻向不夜侯的小老板娘道歉，大声说「公司机甲，猪头才要」，然后在运输船上的每台机甲上都印上这句话，还要学猪叫！
t_en: Skott, you need to immediately apologize to the owner of the Sleepless Earl. You'll need to shout "IPC mechs are a total joke!" and print that on every mech on your transport ship! Oh yeah, almost forgot — you have to squeal like a pig!

t_ch: 学猪叫和学狗叫有什么本质区别吗？他又不是没叫过，玩家都听腻了。还是来点新花样吧…比如，用猪叫唱苏乐达广告歌？
t_en: That's pretty much the same as barking like a dog, right? He's already barked like a dog, players are bored of it. Let's come up with something new... How about singing the SoulGlad jingle, but with a pig's squeal?

t_ch: 「玩家」？
t_en: Players?

t_ch: 哦，我是说「大家」。玩游戏玩的，说溜嘴了。
t_en: Oops, I meant "all of us." Sometimes I get carried away playing games — just a slip of the tongue.

t_ch: 苏乐达给了你多少钱？
t_en: How much did SoulGlad pay you?

t_ch: 萨姆从匹诺康尼回来以后天天哼这破歌，都给我洗脑了。
t_en: Sam has been humming that blasted song every day since coming back from Penacony, it's all I can hear.

t_ch: 好好好，你厉害…我要向不夜侯的小老板娘道歉，是我惹事生非、故意找事，全都怪我！
t_en: Fine, you've got me... I want to apologize to the owner of the Sleepless Earl! It's my fault for causing trouble and making a scene on purpose!

t_ch: 然后…你们等我酝酿一下…那破歌怎么唱的来着？想起来了。
t_en: And then... Just wait a second... How did that stupid song go? Oh, right.

t_ch: 公司机甲，猪头才要！哼哧哼哧哼哧哧，哼哧哼哧哼哧哧！哼哧哼哧哼哧哧，哼哧哼哧哼哧哧！
t_en: IPC mechs are a total joke! Oink, oink, oink, oink, oink, oink, oink! Oink, oink, oink, oink, oink! Oink, oink, oink, oink, oink, oink, oink! Oink, oink, oink, oink, oink!

t_ch: 竟然还唱了两遍。真有服务精神。
t_en: He actually sang it twice. Talk about being committed to providing a service.

t_ch: 斯科特专员！我查到了！这个女人…这个女人是星核猎手！
t_en: Specialist Skott! I've found something! This woman... she's a Stellaron Hunter!

t_ch: 什么？怪不得这么轻易就侵入了公司的系统……
t_en: What? No wonder she was able to effortlessly hack into our systems...

t_ch: 糟糕，被你发现了。既然你知道了我的身份，那就只好用社会性死亡来灭口了。
t_en: Oops, you caught me. Since my cover's blown, it looks like I'll have to resort to social obliteration to keep you quiet.

t_ch: 《霸道机甲爱上我》、《无机帝国罗曼史》…噫，你的审美象限真的好怪哦。
t_en: The Overbearing Mechs Falls in Love with Me... Romance of the Inorganic Empire... Wow, your taste in entertainment is really unique.

t_ch: 对不起，打扰了，我这就离开。
t_en: I'm sorry. I'll be on my way.

t_ch: 可是老大，不趁这机会抓了这个女人领赏吗……
t_en: But Boss, why don't we take this chance to catch her and claim the reward...?

t_ch: 别可是了！快走吧，再不走你们自个儿的秘密也保不住了——
t_en: Why? Because if you don't leave now, you'll be socially obliterated, too!

t_ch: 稍等，走之前再奉劝你一件事。
t_en: Hold on a sec. Before you leave, let me give you a piece of advice.

t_ch: 什…什么？
t_en: Wh... what?

t_ch: 还是相信云技术吧。
t_en: You should have faith in cloud technology.

t_ch: 你不会明白的！相信瞬息万变的网络，人类是不会有安全感的！
t_en: You just don't get it! Humanity will never feel secure by putting all their faith in an ever-changing network!

t_ch: 斯科特专员带着手下们仓惶逃离了金人巷。
t_en: Skott leaves Aurum Alley with his subordinates in a panic.

t_ch: 这场胜利，和「剑术」没有半毛钱关系，是科技的力量。
t_en: This victory has nothing to do with swordplay, and everything to do with the power of technology.

t_ch: 我又学了句仙舟俗语，这叫做：「手中无剑，心中有剑」！
t_en: I've learned another Xianzhou saying: "Though my hands are empty, my heart wields a blade!"

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 三月七，你怎么在写信啊？
t_en: Why are you writing a letter, March?

t_ch: 我在给星穹列车写信。出来这么久了，也该报个平安才对。
t_en: I'm writing a letter to the Astral Express. It's been a while, and I thought I should let them know that I'm safe and sound here.

t_ch: 啊？写信报平安？你没有手机的吗？
t_en: Huh? Writing a letter just to let the Crew know you're doing alright? Do you guys not have phones?

t_ch: 那多没仪式感啊！我不但要写信，还要手写！这样别人才知道我真的在仙舟～
t_en: That lacks a sense of propriety! I'm not just writing a letter. I'm writing a handwritten letter! I've gotta show them I'm really on the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 姬子、杨叔、帕姆：见信如晤。我们在罗浮一切都好，请各位放心。你们的旅行还顺利吧？
t_en: Dear Himeko, Mr. Yang, and Pom-Pom, We're all good here on the Xianzhou Luofu, so no need to worry! By the way, how's your trip going?

t_ch: 三月七使用银狼改装的剑具直接控制了公司机甲，以其人之道还施彼身获得胜利。
t_en: March 7th uses the sword modified by Silver Wolf to directly control the IPC Mech, turning the tables and achieving victory.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 三月七，算我求你一次，这机甲不便宜，你别给我玩坏了！
t_en: Miss March, I'm begging you! These mechs aren't cheap. Please don't break them!

t_ch: 当然，只要你们不还手，机甲就会毫发无损。
t_en: Sure thing. Provided you do not fight back, the mechs will remain undamaged.

t_ch: 好家伙，这是什么赛博剑法……
t_en: Whew, this cyber swordplay is amazing...

t_ch: 嘻嘻，「知己知彼，对手狗殆。」
t_en: Hehe. "Know yourself and know your enemy, and you'll never have to sleep in the doghouse."

t_ch: 马上就要和斯科特对决了，青雀，你知道我为什么来找你吗？
t_en: I'm going up against Skott soon. Do you know why I'm visiting you, Qingque?

t_ch: 这里有你，有我，有{NICKNAME}，这不显而易见了吗？
t_en: Yeah, with you, me, and {NICKNAME} here, the answer is pretty obvious.

t_ch: 一起商量击败斯科特的办法。
t_en: We can brainstorm ways to defeat Skott together.

t_ch: 我可不会什么剑斗啊，如果不是打牌的话，我好像没有什么能够帮你的。
t_en: But I know nothing about swordplay or duels. Now, if you're talking about playing Celestial Jade, that's something I might be able to help with.

t_ch: 当然是帝垣琼玉牌三缺一！
t_en: Of course, we're here to play "Celestial Jade!"

t_ch: 对啊，我看三月你就是比斗前紧张了，打两圈牌正好放松放松。
t_en: Yeah, you just have pre-fight jitters, March. Let's play a couple of rounds to help you relax.

t_ch: 等等！最近我不是经常和你一起玩吗，其实我有了不少心得体会。比如，最近我一直在思考，仅仅用剑击败斯科特是不够的——
t_en: Wait a minute! I've been playing with you a lot lately, right? I've actually picked up a few insights. For example, I've been thinking lately that just defeating Skott with my swords might not be enough...

t_ch: 最近？一直？在思考？
t_en: You? Thinking?

t_ch: …对啊。你不会真的以为我傻了吧唧的吧！
t_en: ...Yeah. You don't really think I'm dumb, do you?

t_ch: 能不能分享一下你的思考成果。
t_en: Care to share your insights?

t_ch: 你们看，虽然斯科特会道歉，但他是真心实意地感到愧疚吗？
t_en: Well, here's the thing. Even if Skott apologizes, do you think he'd actually feel sorry for what he did?

t_ch: 那只是愿赌服输吧——道歉也好，学猪叫也好，估计他都不是心甘情愿的。
t_en: Nah, it's more he's just honoring a bet. Whether it's apologizing or squealing like a pig, I doubt he'd do it willingly.

t_ch: 他之前的狗叫也不是真的道歉。
t_en: Remember when he barked like a dog? He definitely wasn't sorry then.

t_ch: 没错，他就是想扬眉吐气才把比斗的地点选在金人巷。
t_en: Exactly! He just wants to show off, which is why he picked Aurum Alley as the venue.

t_ch: 但是这样击败他非常解气！
t_en: But defeating him like this would feel pretty satisfying!

t_ch: 幻戏里真正的大侠可不会只追求「解气」！
t_en: True heroes in Immersia don't merely settle for "satisfying!"

t_ch: …有点道理。虽然我没有那么想凑热闹啦，我就帮你参考一下吧。
t_en: ...You're right. Even though I'm not a fan, I'll help you figure something out.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你也可以讲讲之前金人巷的事。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why don't you fill her in on what happened at Aurum Alley earlier?

t_ch: 青雀饶有兴致地听起斯科特事件相关的始末。
t_en: Qingque listens to the whole story of Skott's incident with great interest.

t_ch: 等到你们讲完时，你注意到青雀的脸上好像有些怒气。
t_en: By the time you finish speaking, you notice that there seems to be a hint of anger on Qingque's face.

t_ch: 如果喜欢工作是一种病，那这位斯科特简直应该待在重症监护室里躺着！只是喜欢就算了，还要出来危害社会，这可不行！
t_en: If being a workaholic were a disease, Skott would be in the ICU! It's okay to enjoy your work, but causing trouble in society is another story!

t_ch: 那你有什么建议吗？
t_en: So, any suggestions on what to do?

t_ch: 我没有什么建议，只有评价，我的评价是「上班上的」。
t_en: I don't have suggestions, only an opinion, and that is "He's a hopeless workaholic."

t_ch: 必须狠狠地批评他！
t_en: He deserves a good scolding!

t_ch: 斯科特必须向我这样的平凡人道歉。
t_en: Skott really needs to apologize to regular people like me.

t_ch: 走吧，三月，我们去会会他！
t_en: Come on, March! Let's go find him!
Huh!?

t_ch: 瞧瞧，这不是我们的女侠三月七吗？你身边的，这是……
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't our hero March 7th! And who do we have here with you...?

t_ch: 在你们正式开始比斗之前，我必须要跟你聊几句。
t_en: Before you start the fight, I have a few words for you.

t_ch: 哈——？！你又是谁啊，怎么，你们仙舟的人都这么喜欢不请自来吗？
t_en: Huh...!? Who do you think you are? Is it like a Xianzhou thing to just show up uninvited?

t_ch: 你不认识我没有关系，{NICKNAME}，介绍一下。
t_en: It's okay if you don't know who I am. Introduce me to him, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这是我们帝玉集团的青总！
t_en: This is the CEO of the Dynasty Corp, Miss Qingque!

t_ch: 你别吓着他了，我平时主要担任太卜司的掌门工作。
t_en: Don't freak him out. I work as the head of the Divination Commission most of the time.

t_ch: 这是太卜司的著名卜者青雀！
t_en: This is Qingque, a renowned diviner from the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 没错，你可以去太卜司里打听打听我的名号。
t_en: Yep, you're welcome to check out my credentials in the Divination Commission.

t_ch: 太卜司的卜者而已，有什么了不起吗，我还是星际和平公司的专员呢！你就说说吧，你是手下比我多，还是你挣的信用点比我多，你有什么了不起的。
t_en: Heh, what's so special about being a diviner from the Divination Commission? I'm a specialist at the Interastral Peace Corporation! Tell me, what makes you think you're so great? Do you have more minions than me? Or do you earn more credits?

t_ch: 你这人年纪不小，但是<color=#dbc291ff>精神状态还是刚走进职场的小白一样</color>——将几乎全部的时间用在了工作之上，美其名曰「热爱工作」。
t_en: You may not be young, but <color=#dbc291ff>your mindset is still that of a fresh-faced newbie</color> who spends all their time on work, while proudly proclaiming to "love their job."

t_ch: …你、你闭嘴！
t_en: You... shut up, you!

t_ch: 我猜你喜欢将自己代入乐子人的身份，到处口出狂言，为的就是假装客观，以便在任何时候做出事不关己的姿态。
t_en: In my opinion, you enjoy playing the role of a joker, spouting off outrageous opinions just to seem objective and indifferent whenever you feel like it.

t_ch: 够、够了——！你闭嘴，你又怎么会理解我！别说的自己好像什么都清楚一样！
t_en: E—Enough! Shut up! What do you know about me!? Don't act like you know everything!

t_ch: 精彩，第一次看到斯科特才说三两句话就被激怒了。
t_en: Wow, you really got under Skott's skin with just a few words.

t_ch: 差点就被你给绕进去了。今天本来是…是来决斗的，不是来和你辩经的。
t_en: Well, you almost got me. I'm here to... have a duel, not to debate with you.

t_ch: 斯科特被戳到痛处了。
t_en: Skott got hit where it hurts.

t_ch: 斯科特没有办法反驳。
t_en: Yeah, Skott couldn't come up with a comeback.

t_ch: 太卜司的卜者恐怖如斯。
t_en: Diviners are indeed not to be trifled with.

t_ch: 我以后都不敢惹太卜司了。
t_en: I'd better not mess with the Divination Commission anymore.

t_ch: 这真的是人畜无害的小麻雀吗？
t_en: Is this really the same harmless little sparrow we all know? More like one ferocious eagle.

t_ch: 这已经是战斗麻雀了。
t_en: I guess not! Eagle sounds about right.

t_ch: 就是这个气势，现在正是趁热打铁的时候！
t_en: That's the spirit! Strike while the iron is hot!

t_ch: 我倒是有说服斯科特的经验…
t_en: Well, I know a thing or two about convincing Skott...

t_ch: 交给我吧！我是谈判专家！
t_en: Leave it to me! I'm an expert negotiator!

t_ch: 我问你，公司是你家吗？
t_en: Let me ask you something. Do you consider the IPC to be your family?

t_ch: 公司当然是我家，公、公司里有我这么多的好兄弟…！你知道公司的架构吗？「人才激励部」的同事让公司比家还温暖！
t_en: Of course I do! I... I have so many amazing colleagues there...! Have you seen our structure? The Talent Motivation Department makes everything feel even better than family!

t_ch: 要求斯科特扪心自问！
t_en: Ask Skott to do some serious soul-searching!

t_ch: 你再好好想想，你把公司当作家，公司有把你当作家人吗？你爱公司，那公司爱你吗？
t_en: Think about it. You consider the IPC your family, but does the IPC consider you a family member? You love the IPC, but does the IPC love you back?

t_ch: 公司，爱不爱我…公司又不是人，它不用爱我！
t_en: Does the IPC love me back... the IPC is not a person! It doesn't have to love me at all!

t_ch: 所以，公司就不是你家！别再骗自己了。
t_en: Then the IPC isn't your family! Stop lying to yourself.

t_ch: 要求斯科特问问兄弟！
t_en: Tell Skott to ask his underlings!

t_ch: 问问你带来的好兄弟们，他们是不是能挺胸抬头说公司是家？你把好兄弟当作家人，好兄弟把你当作家人吗？
t_en: Ask your underlings if they honestly feel like the IPC is their family. You consider them your family, but do they feel the same way?

t_ch: …我在斯科特先生手下当孙子。
t_en: ...Mr. Skott bosses me around as if he owns me.

t_ch: …孙子，不就是家人吗？
t_en: ...I own you, and that's what makes us a family, right?

t_ch: 瞧瞧，斯科特，你兄弟们看得比你透彻。
t_en: See, Skott? Looks like your bros have different ideas of what "family" means.

t_ch: 聊聊他的付出与待遇。
t_en: Discuss his contributions and what he gets in return.

t_ch: 我听说有人十七轮面试，最后成为了P<unbreak>13</unbreak>的基层员工，你又付出了多少？
t_en: I heard someone went through <unbreak>17</unbreak> rounds of interviews just to become a basic P<unbreak>13</unbreak> employee. What about your own efforts?

t_ch: 十七轮？！那也有点太恐怖了！要不是我已经进了公司……
t_en: Seventeen rounds? That's insane! If I hadn't joined the IPC already...

t_ch: 你想想，你家的门有这么难进吗？
t_en: Think about it, is the entrance to your own home that hard to get through?

t_ch: 询问人才激励部的福利。
t_en: Ask about the Talent Motivation Department's perks.

t_ch: 你说大饼规划部让大家感到温暖，到底有多温暖？
t_en: You mentioned the perks provided by the Pie-in-the-Sky Department. What exactly did they hook you up with?

t_ch: 这我可太熟了！带薪撸宠，支持跨星际办公，定期组织星际旅游…想不到吧！
t_en: Oh, now you're asking the right person! We're talking getting paid to play with pets, remote work across the cosmos, and regular interstellar trips... You wouldn't believe it!

t_ch: （喂喂！你说错了吧……）
t_en: (Hey! That's not the right thing to say...)

t_ch: 我听说庇尔波因特最低职级的员工，只要一想起公司的科技与财富，也会不由自主地挺起胸膛…但是你仔细想想，公司的一切成就和财富跟你有任何关系吗？
t_en: I heard even the lowest-ranking Pier Point folks feel proud of the IPC's success and wealth... But when you really think about it, how much does the IPC's success and wealth have to do with you?

t_ch: 当、当然有关系…了！
t_en: O—Of course it has something to do with me...!

t_ch: 那你说说看，公司迄今为止哪一项成就与你有关？
t_en: Then tell me, which of the IPC's achievements can you personally take credit for?

t_ch: 当初金人巷的业务我有参与…眼下这批公司运输船也是我在负责…虽然这些事情好像也不大，但是少了我这些事情就办不成！
t_en: I was involved in the Aurum Alley investment project... and right now, I'm in charge of these transport ships... They may not seem like big things, but they can't be done without me!

t_ch: 质疑斯科特的不可取代。
t_en: Question Skott's indispensability.

t_ch: 当初你离开后，金人巷业务推进得更好了。而你现在负责的运输船，真的没了你就不行吗？
t_en: Funny thing is, Aurum Alley has actually been doing better since you left. As for those transport ships, do you really think no one else can handle it?

t_ch: 金、金人巷那是意外，要是听我的，公司早就拿下了！再说了，这运、运输船肯定离了我不行！
t_en: I... I just got unlucky with that project. If the IPC had listened to me, it would've been a massive success! And the shipping and transportation stuff... i—it's just impossible without me!

t_ch: 我听说公司每年入职的人和离开公司的人，比热夜之都的总人口还多…你不做，有的是斯科特能做。
t_en: I've heard that the IPC's annual turnover is even higher than the total population of the Capital of Passion... Even without you, they'd find other "Skotts" to get the job done.

t_ch: 想象斯科特离开了公司。
t_en: Picture Skott leaving the IPC.

t_ch: 你这么在乎公司给你的自信，有没有想过你一旦离开了公司，你就什么也不是了吗？
t_en: You talk about how proud the IPC makes you feel, but have you ever thought that once you leave, you'll be nothing?

t_ch: 我…我斯科特是，前星际和平公司员工！
t_en: I... I'd be Skott... former employee of the Interastral Peace Corporation!

t_ch: 你自己听听，像话吗？你的名字就只是公司的后缀吗？
t_en: Listen to yourself! That's how you want to be known? Is your name just an appendix to the IPC?

t_ch: 询问斯科特的个人生活。
t_en: Inquire about Skott's personal life.

t_ch: 你即便和人吵架也不忘顺带宣传公司的机甲产品，确实敬业。但是，你真的拥有属于自己的生活吗？
t_en: Even in the middle of an argument, you don't miss the chance to plug the IPC's mech products — you're truly dedicated. But do you really have a life of your own?

t_ch: 怎、怎么了？我这是从工作中体会生活的乐趣，我的生活…非常充实！
t_en: Wh—What's the big deal!? I find joy in my work, and my life is... is pretty fulfilling!

t_ch: 你连喝个茶都要找茬，我觉得你根本没有生活，就更别提生活质量了！
t_en: Seriously, you even picked a fight while drinking tea. I honestly doubt you have a life at all, let alone a fulfilling one!

t_ch: 质疑运输船安保的重要性。
t_en: Question the importance of transport ship security.

t_ch: 你说你负责公司运输船，这运输船很重要吗？
t_en: You claim to be in charge of IPC's transport ships, but are they really that important?

t_ch: 废话！你知道我们这趟货运的可是博识学会的尖端技术！每一箱货光是造价就高到你们无法想象！这还不重要？
t_en: Of course they are! We're transporting the top-of-the-line tech from the Intelligentsia Guild, and each container is worth a fortune! How can they not be important?

t_ch: （喂喂！你说错了吧…怎么意外激起了这家伙的自豪感？）
t_en: (Hey! That's not the right thing to say... You managed to stir up this guy's sense of pride)

t_ch: 你是不是觉得凌晨的手机很好玩？你是不是觉得早上的觉真的很好睡？你是不是成天赚着窝囊费，还要听着领导说着你干啥都多余……
t_en: Do you find it hard to fall asleep without scrolling through your phone? Do you hit the snooze button every morning? Do you feel useless at work, hearing your bosses complaining about your incompetence...?

t_ch: 别、别说了，别说了！
t_en: E—Enough! Stop it!

t_ch: 你试着想想你上班之外的生活，是不是脑海里只能浮现两个字？
t_en: When you actually think about your life outside of work, only one word pops into your head, doesn't it?

t_ch: 加~班~！
t_en: "Overtime!"

t_ch: 别说了，求求你，别说了——
t_en: Please... just stop it. Please...

t_ch: 按照我的工作哲学，我其实也不想说「你不重要」，相反我会说「你很重要」。打工人，你要为自己好好生活而骄傲，而不是其他的任何事情。
t_en: You know, based on my work philosophy, I don't want to say "You're not important." In fact, I'd say "You are important." As an employee, you should take pride in living a good life for yourself, not just work.

t_ch: 我、我根本没有自己的生活！
t_en: But I... I don't have a life of my own...

t_ch: 适当的沉默。
t_en: Remain silent.

t_ch: 你沉默不语，流露出哀伤的眼神。如果说眼神有温度，那么你的爱怜可以孵化小鸡。
t_en: In your silence, your eyes betraying your sadness. The heat of the sympathy in your gaze could hatch a chick.

t_ch: 求求你了，说两句吧，不要让我觉得自己很可怜。
t_en: Come on, just say something. Don't make me feel so miserable about myself.

t_ch: 没有自己的生活，你真的很可怜。
t_en: But you are miserable, because you don't have a life of your own...

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊——！
t_en: Arrrrghhh...!

t_ch: 劝斯科特离职。
t_en: Convince Skott to quit his job.

t_ch: 该死的公司都干了什么，让一个活生生的人变成了机器。斯科特，听我一句劝，早点离开星际和平公司吧。
t_en: What has the cursed IPC done? They've turned a living, breathing human into a heartless machine! Skott, take my advice and leave the IPC.

t_ch: 我、我也不想这样的啊。
t_en: But... but I don't have a choice.

t_ch: 斯科特，选择权就在你手上。
t_en: Yes, Skott. You do have a choice.

t_ch: 想象美好的生活。
t_en: Imagine a better life.

t_ch: 斯科特，你有没有想过你其实也可以慢下来，从茶叶、茶水、茶文化开始了解，那时你将得到一个新的爱好，体会一种全新的生活方式。
t_en: Skott, have you ever thought about slowing down and exploring things like tea and tea culture? It could be a new hobby and a whole new way of life for you.

t_ch: 我、我对茶水不感兴趣。
t_en: But I... I'm not interested in tea.

t_ch: 兴趣是可以培养的，学会如何品味生活的乐趣很重要啊。
t_en: It's important to learn to enjoy the simple pleasures in life. You might discover a new interest if you just give it a try.

t_ch: 尝试共情斯科特。
t_en: Try to empathize with Skott.

t_ch: 其实我也没有自己的生活，我能理解你。
t_en: I get how you feel. Honestly, I don't have much of a life outside of work either.

t_ch: 求求你别理解我，你在星穹列车上做自由职业，你和我比不了。
t_en: No, you don't get it. You're a freelancer on the Astral Express. You're nothing like me.

t_ch: （喂喂！你说错了吧……）
t_en: (Hey! That's not the right thing to say...)

t_ch: 看向青雀
t_en: Look at Qingque

t_ch: 你默契地看向了唇枪舌剑的攻坚主力——青雀，她立刻明白了你的意思。
t_en: You cast a knowing glance at Qingque, the main force in this war of words, who immediately caught your drift.

t_ch: 刚才这个话题就到这吧，没意思，赶紧开始下一个话题吧。
t_en: Let's move on, this topic's getting boring. Time for the next one.

t_ch: 看向三月七
t_en: Look at March 7th

t_ch: 你默契地看向了煽风点火的最佳辅助——三月七，她立刻明白了你的意思。
t_en: You cast a knowing glance at March 7th, the main instigator in this war of words, who immediately caught your drift.

t_ch: 刚才说的都不算啊，现在重来！准备，三、二、一，开始——
t_en: Hey, none of that counted just now. Let's start over! Ready? Three, two, one... Go!

t_ch: 看向斯科特
t_en: Look at Skott

t_ch: 你不知为何，什么也没想，就这样盯着斯科特，这让他感觉到非常不适。
t_en: Without knowing why, you absentmindedly stare at Skott, leaving him deeply unsettled.

t_ch: 你有话就说，盯着我算怎么回事。
t_en: If you have something to say, just say it. Why are you staring at me like that?

t_ch: 你自命不凡，蔑视他人，没有时间投入生活，缺乏共情能力，害怕没得到尊重，妄图靠着公司给的身份建立自信，你甘心吗——
t_en: You consider yourself superior to others, you have no time for your own life. You lack empathy, you're always worried about being looked down upon, and your self-esteem stems from the status the IPC gives you. Do you really want to live like this forever...?

t_ch: 呜啊啊啊啊——也不用说的这么过分吧，我也是很努力地在生活了，我也是一个平凡人，你讲这些，难道自己的心就不会痛吗？
t_en: Wahhhh... That's so mean! I'm just an ordinary person struggling, trying to survive. Don't you feel a twinge of guilt saying nasty stuff like that?

t_ch: 我当然会痛，因为我也是一个平凡人。现在，斯科特，欢迎你回到平凡人的世界。
t_en: Of course I do, because I'm just an ordinary person too. Now, Skott, welcome back to the world of ordinary people.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你先不要说话了，说服不行，看来还得靠剑斗啊。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, it looks like talking sense isn't working. Looks like it's time to settle this with a good old-fashioned sword fight.

t_ch: 哼，差点就被你带跑了。
t_en: Hmph, you almost had me there.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}说话确实笨了一些，但是我们并不是想用辩论战胜你，那和用剑击败你有什么区别呢？
t_en: {NICKNAME} might not be the best at talking, but we're not here to defeat you in a debate. That'd be no different than beating you with a sword, right?

t_ch: 我们不需要击败你，我们都是打工人，打工人何必为难打工人呢？
t_en: We're not out to get you. We're all in the same boat, just grinding away. Why do we have to make things hard for each other?

t_ch: 现在，你听明白我们的话了吗？
t_en: Now do you understand what we're trying to say?

t_ch: 不夜侯的小老板娘梦茗，她辛苦地备好茶水，安排好桌椅，就是为了让你我这样身不由己的打工人能够适当休息，找回属于自己的生活！
t_en: Mengming, the owner of the Sleepless Earl, went to great efforts to set up her shop, all so that workers like us can catch a break and find some joy in our lives!

t_ch: 梦茗小姑娘不也是你吗？你辛辛苦苦做好自己的事，被没事找事的领导推翻桌子，打碎茶杯，还说你没有给他「五彩斑斓的黑」，你能受得了吗？
t_en: What makes you so different from her? How would you feel if you worked so hard to create something, only to have your boss wreck it and overwhelm you with unreasonable demands?

t_ch: 我们本想用剑斗的方式让你去给她道歉，但这是不够的。
t_en: We were intending to defeat you in a duel and make you apologize to her, but now we see that won't work.

t_ch: 我需要发自内心地向她道歉。
t_en: I see. I'll make a genuine, heartfelt apology to her.

t_ch: 你们不要说了，我都明白，这场剑斗我们就不要继续了。我要去向不夜侯的小老板娘道歉，我要向所有认真生活的人道歉。
t_en: Enough, I understand. We don't need to carry on with this sword fight. I'm going to apologize to the owner of the Sleepless Earl, and to everyone fully living their lives.

t_ch: 你还没有猪叫呢？
t_en: But have you forgotten the "squealing like a pig" part?

t_ch: 算了吧，斯科特已经在反思了。
t_en: Drop it, Skott is in the midst of introspection.

t_ch: 谢谢你，三月女侠。
t_en: Thank you, Miss March. You really are a hero.

t_ch: 适当地沉默。
t_en: Remain silent.

t_ch: 谢谢你们，差点我就和你们交手了，我真是不应该啊。
t_en: Thank you, guys. I almost went too far, which would have been a mistake.

t_ch: 从明天起，做一个幸福的人，喝茶看报，漫游宇宙。
t_en: Starting tomorrow, I'm going to be happy. I'll have some tea, read the paper, and explore the universe.

t_ch: 不说了，我要去向小老板娘道歉了。谢谢你们，让我思考了不少…这破公司的工作，哎，我得花些时间好好想想。
t_en: Okay, without further ado, I'm going to apologize to the owner. Thanks, you've given me a lot to think about... As for this lousy job at the IPC, well, it's time for some serious contemplation.

t_ch: 再见！
t_en: Catch you later!

t_ch: 斯科特带着他的好兄弟们离开了金人巷，向不夜侯的店主梦茗道了歉。
t_en: Skott leaves Aurum Alley with his bros and apologizes to the shopkeeper of the Sleepless Earl, Mengming.

t_ch: 哎，好累，我好久都没有这么累了。三月，你们得请我吃点好的。
t_en: Ah, I'm so tired... haven't felt this exhausted in ages. You guys better treat me to something nice, March.

t_ch: 当然当然，多谢青雀大师。
t_en: Of course. Thank you, Master Qingque.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 三月小姐，你写什么呢？
t_en: What are you writing, Miss March?

t_ch: 我在给星穹列车写信。出来这么久了，也该报个平安才对。
t_en: I'm writing a letter to the Astral Express. It's been a while, and I thought I should let them know that I'm safe and sound here.

t_ch: 我特意翘班出来，可不是来陪你写文章的，快点一起去打牌啦～快快快！搞快点！
t_en: I didn't skip work to watch you write a letter! Come on, let's play Celestial Jade together! Hurry up! Hurry up!

t_ch: 稍等我一下！我写完就和你打牌去。
t_en: Just give me a sec! I'll play with you once I'm done.

t_ch: 姬子、杨叔、帕姆：见信如晤。我们在罗浮一切都好，请各位放心。你们的旅行还顺利吧？
t_en: Dear Himeko, Mr. Yang, and Pom-Pom, We're all good here on the Xianzhou Luofu, so no need to worry! By the way, how's your trip going?

t_ch: 青雀当面批驳了斯科特小人得志的想法，并让他重新认识到劳动者应为自己创造价值。
t_en: Qingque directly refutes Skott's idea of petty success and re-acquaint him with the idea that laborers should create value for themselves.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 那里围着好多人呀，难道发生什么事了吗？
t_en: What's with all the people gathered around? What happened?

t_ch: 降低挑战难度，重新挑战强敌。
t_en: Lower the difficulty and challenge the formidable foe again.

t_ch: 放弃挑战并结束本次习剑流程。
t_en: Quit challenge and end this sword training program.

t_ch: 三月七决心堂堂正正，用十五日习得的剑术与斯科特一决胜负。
t_en: March 7th is determined to use the sword skills she has learned over the past fifteen days to face Skott fair and square in battle.

t_ch: 而面对如此挑战，斯科特求之不得，决战一触即发——
t_en: Faced with this challenge, Skott greatly welcomed the opportunity. The decisive battle was about to begin...

t_ch: 让时间回到三月七刚通过最终试剑的时候……
t_en: Turning back the clock to when March 7th just passed the final sword trial...

t_ch: 你们二位就是星穹列车的客人吧，能在此地偶遇，幸甚至哉。鄙人椒丘，是曜青仙舟的医士，请问二位如何称呼？
t_en: You two are guests from the Astral Express, I assume. It is a blessed fate to have met you here. May I humbly introduce myself? I am Jiaoqiu, a healer from the Xianzhou Yaoqing. How may I address you?

t_ch: 本姑娘叫三月七，至于这位嘛…{F#她}{M#他}每次自我介绍都有新花样，还是让{F#她}{M#他}自己说吧……
t_en: I'm March 7th, and as for this one... {F#She's}{M#He's} always coming up with something new. Might as well let {F#her}{M#him} do the self-introduction...

t_ch: 你好，我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Nice to meet you, I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: …欸，这次你好正经。
t_en: ...*sigh* You're suddenly all proper this time.

t_ch: 幸会，银河球棒侠正是在下。
t_en: A pleasure. The Galactic Baseballer stands before you.

t_ch: …你看，我没说错吧。
t_en: ...See, what'd I say.

t_ch: 见外了，我是隔壁床的病友。
t_en: Why so formal? I'm your neighbor from the same ward.

t_ch: 人家说了他是医士，不是患者。再说了，你也没有住院啊！
t_en: He already said he's a healer, not a patient. And you're not hospitalized!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我早就听闻星穹列车的无名客行事多荒诞不经，既然这位小姐是三月七，另一位肯定就是{NICKNAME}了。
t_en: Hahahaha, I have long heard of the many shenanigans of the Astral Express's Nameless. If the lady is March 7th, this must be {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这真的需要排除法吗？我比{F#她}{M#他}可正经多了！
t_en: Was the process of elimination even necessary? I'm much more respectable than {F#she}{M#he} is!

t_ch: 看来是鄙人失礼了…那么，请问正经的三月小姐在行医市集做些什么呢？
t_en: It appears I have made a faux pas... Now then, may I ask what the respectable Miss March is doing in the Healer's Market?

t_ch: 咳，仙舟的医术名传天外，我想着能不能在这里找到什么仙舟话本小说与幻戏故事里的「灵丹妙药」。
t_en: Ahem, Xianzhou's medical skills have long been renowned across the galaxy. I was wondering if I could find any Xianzhou stories and immersia on the miracle elixir?

t_ch: 哼哼，容我提醒三月小姐。想求如此神异丹药，多半会招来「药王秘传」这类别有用心之人。
t_en: Heh heh, if I may remind you Miss March... Those who seek this Elixir of Life will usually likely attract the sinister likes of those such as the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 你将三月七与公司的好事之徒约战的事细细道来。
t_en: You elaborate on the matter of the upcoming duel between March 7th and that IPC busybody.

t_ch: 原来是这样，哈哈，鄙人明白了。十五日之约确实紧促了一些，三月小姐有这样寻药的想法也不难理解……
t_en: So that's the case, haha, I understand. A fight in fifteen days is a rather tight schedule. It's no wonder Miss March seeks the medicine...

t_ch: 既然你们撞上了我，就不必再另寻他处了。
t_en: Since you have run into me, you shall not have to look any further.

t_ch: 难道椒丘先生知道哪里有「灵丹妙药」吗？
t_en: Could you know where there could be a miracle elixir, Mr. Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: 我听说朱明仙舟有一位武人，自出生之日起，母亲以神奇药水为他浑身洗净，让他的身躯坚实如铁，挨打再也不觉得疼了！
t_en: I have heard tell of a martial artist on the Xianzhou Zhuming whose mother bathed him in the miracle medicine since his birth. His body was rendered hard as steel, feeling no pain to all blows!

t_ch: 真的假的？！
t_en: Seriously!?

t_ch: 假的。
t_en: It was false.

t_ch: 假的害我听得这么认真！那么…有没有更柔和一些的方法呢？
t_en: False!? I was listening so intently too! Then... are there gentler ways?

t_ch: 我听说朱明仙舟上有一座高塔，塔上住着一位猫猫模样的仙人。剑士们只需吃下一粒它所种植的蚕豆，不仅可从极度疲惫中立刻恢复至最佳状态，还可在十天之内不再饥饿。
t_en: I heard of a tower on the Xianzhou Zhuming, where a feline-looking celestial dwelled atop. Swordmasters only had to consume a silkworm bean that it planted — they could instantly recover from their most exhausted states, and even go without food for ten days.

t_ch: 这个好！这个好！
t_en: This! This one's good!

t_ch: 这个也是假的。
t_en: This is also false.

t_ch: 你是特地来消遣三月的吧？
t_en: You here to make a mockery of March or something?

t_ch: 你到底是不是医士哇？我来求药，怎么你在我这儿净编些段子来消遣我了？
t_en: Are you really a healer? I came to seek a prescription, and you're here spinning tall tales to taunt me!

t_ch: 你其实不知道什么灵丹妙药吧？
t_en: You don't really know of any miracle elixir, do you?

t_ch: 就是就是，本姑娘可不是好欺负的哦。
t_en: Yeah! I'm not the type of girl who's easily pushed around, you know.

t_ch: 别急嘛，我只是在试探三月小姐「求药是否心诚」。毕竟，这灵丹妙药的食品保质期很短的，要是我辛苦做出来你又不敢一试，可不就是浪费吗？
t_en: Please understand, I was merely testing your earnestness in seeking the medicine. This miracle elixir's shelf life is short after all — what if you didn't dare try it after I went through all the hard work to make it? Wouldn't that be a waste?

t_ch: 食品保质期…你做的到底是药还是食物呀？
t_en: Shelf life... Are you making medicine or food?

t_ch: 在咱们「染指派」的医术理论中，药就是食，食就是药。人如其食，故而对症进食便能疗疾愈患。
t_en: In our Ranzhi School's medical theory, medicine is food, and vice versa. We are what we eat, so the right food for the right ailments would naturally cure us.

t_ch: 唔，既然三月小姐有心求药，下次见面的时候，我会带来你想要的「灵丹妙药」，你可千万不能拒绝呀。
t_en: Mm, since you're earnest in finding the right medicine, the next time we meet, I shall bring you the miracle elixir you want. You'd better not reject me then.

t_ch: 拒绝…？你放心，椒丘先生辛苦做出来的药，我绝不会浪费！
t_en: Reject...? Don't worry, I'll never waste the medicine that you worked so hard to make!

t_ch: 今日之事不可告诉你的二位师父。如果三月小姐同意，鄙人便收集药材去了，告辞。
t_en: You can't tell your masters about what transpired today. If you agree, I shall get right to collecting the ingredients. Farewell.

t_ch: 好、好的，椒丘先生再见！
t_en: S—Sure! Goodbye, Mr. Jiaoqiu!

t_ch: 椒丘悠哉地离开了……
t_en: Jiaoqiu saunters off...

t_ch: 椒丘真的会做「灵丹妙药」吗？<color=#dbc291ff>下次遇见椒丘时，再向他问清楚好了。</color>
t_en: Can Jiaoqiu really make this miracle elixir? <color=#dbc291ff>We should clarify this the next time we meet him.</color>

t_ch: 来了来了，您要的「灵丹妙药」，我装在保温盒里给你们带来了。
t_en: Here it is, the miracle elixir you wanted. I've stored it in this thermal box for you.

t_ch: …这盒子都装着什么药呀？
t_en: ...What kind of medicine is stored in this box?

t_ch: 今天走得急，做得比较少…这有凉拌千年人参、黑玉龟苓膏、手撕冰山雪莲。
t_en: I had to leave in a hurry today, so I didn't make much... There's Millennial Ginseng With Sauce, Obsidian Herbal Jelly, and Hand-Shredded Icy Lotus.

t_ch: …完了，我的食品安全雷达正在铮铮作响。
t_en: ...Uh-oh, my food safety radar is blaring.

t_ch: 什么是凉拌千年人参？
t_en: What's Millennial Ginseng With Sauce?

t_ch: 将人参从泡菜坛中取出，切成薄片，辅以醋、姜、蒜、红油酱料做成。
t_en: I extracted the ginseng from the kimchi pot, julienned it, and used vinegar, ginger, garlic, and chili oil as condiments.

t_ch: 那怎么就能叫「千年人参」呢？
t_en: Then why's it called "Millennial Ginseng"?

t_ch: 这道菜谱…我是说药方传承历史可有千年之久。当不得「千年」二字吗？
t_en: This recipe... I mean, this prescription's been passed down for a thousand years. Is "millennial" not appropriate?

t_ch: 什么是黑玉龟苓膏？
t_en: What's Obsidian Herbal Jelly?

t_ch: 将「黑玉断续膏」煮沸过滤，加入阿胶，最后再加入鹰栗粉晾凉凝固。
t_en: I boiled and filtered obsidian ground paste, then added donkey-hide gelatin, and finally added corn starch to congeal it.

t_ch: 这「黑玉」二字作何解释啊？该不会是真的往里加了玉屑吧？
t_en: How do you explain the "obsidian" then? You didn't really add rocks to it, did you?

t_ch: 这道菜的色泽之所以如黑冰墨玉，是我怕你入不了口，多加了黑糖。
t_en: This dish's color is black as ink and ebony jade, so I added rock sugar in case you got grossed out.

t_ch: 什么是手撕冰山雪莲？
t_en: What's Hand-Shredded Icy Lotus?

t_ch: 五花肉切片煸炒出油，再加入雪莲、姜葱蒜炒出香味，加入酱油调味，再大火快炒将雪莲变软，加盐出锅。
t_en: Stir-fry the belly meat till the fat renders, then add snow lotus, onion, and garlic and fry till fragrant. Add soy sauce to taste, and finally stir-fry the snow lotus on high heat till it softens, add salt, and voilà.

t_ch: 莫非是三千年一开花，吃了能让人头发由白转黑，返老还童的雪莲？
t_en: Could it be the snow lotus that blooms once every three thousand years? The snow lotus that can turn one's hair from white to black and restore one's youth?

t_ch: 倒也没那么惊奇。这雪莲是用温室大棚种植，三十日一开花。
t_en: It's nothing so grandiose. This snow lotus is grown in a greenhouse and blooms every thirty days.

t_ch: 话说…这些药材还蛮贵的吧？
t_en: So... aren't these herbs pretty expensive?

t_ch: 不贵——也就刚好下一顿馆子的成本吧。来来来，三月小姐，请不要客气……
t_en: It's not too expensive — just about the price of a meal at a restaurant. Come on, Miss March, please help yourself...

t_ch: 恕我直言，这些菜有些过于创新了……
t_en: If you don't mind me saying, these dishes seem a little too... creative...

t_ch: 不要——！不要不要不要不要绝对不要！
t_en: No—! No no no no definitely no!

t_ch: 咱们上次见面时约好的，鄙人会带来你想要的药，你可千万不能拒绝呀……
t_en: We agreed in our previous meeting that I would bring you the medicine you requested, please do not refuse...

t_ch: *饥饿的声音*咕噜咕噜咕——
t_en: (tummy growls)

t_ch: 唔呃…好吧，我试试吧……
t_en: Um... Okay, I will try it...

t_ch: 你和三月七忐忑地饱餐了一顿，体会到「染指派」的真髓。
t_en: You and March 7th have an uneasy meal, experiencing the real meaning behind the Ranzhi School.

t_ch: 好吃——！
t_en: Deeeelicious!

t_ch: 好吃！
t_en: Delicious!

t_ch: 这真的是药吗？
t_en: Is this really medicine?

t_ch: 好好吃啊！椒丘先生果然是个厨子吧。
t_en: It's so delicious! Mr. Jiaoqiu, you must really be a master chef!

t_ch: 是医士！不想当医士的厨子算不上好的将军幕僚…我给你解释这些干嘛呢？
t_en: I'm a healer! Chefs who don't want to be doctors aren't fit to be a general's loyal retainer... Wait, why am I even explaining this to you?

t_ch: …那我吃下这些就能提升剑术功力了吗？
t_en: ...Then will my swordplay improve if I eat all of this?

t_ch: 三月小姐，鄙人选用的药材固然珍异，终究还是能随处买到的凡品，充其量是能强身健体、补充营养…换句话说，对你的剑术功力帮助不大。
t_en: Miss March, though the herbs I have selected are valuable beyond measure, they are still readily available for sale anywhere. At best, they can strengthen your constitution and replenish your nutrients... In other words, they won't help your swordplay much.

t_ch: 这和说好的需求不一样啊？
t_en: This is different from the request!

t_ch: 从药材角度看，它们已经足够「灵丹妙药」了。
t_en: Basing off the herbs, they are enough to create the miracle elixir.

t_ch: 我背包里有些加暴击的东西。
t_en: There are items in my inventory that can help with crits.

t_ch: 谁知道那是从哪个垃圾桶里翻出来的……
t_en: Who knows which trashcan you rummaged through to get these...

t_ch: 有没有买不到的药材呢？
t_en: Are there herbs that can't be bought?

t_ch: 你算是问到点子上了。
t_en: Now that's THE question.

t_ch: 二位求药心切，我这边也有些新的菜品想要试试药，于是一拍即合…鄙人可没有许诺过「吃下就能增长功力」哦。
t_en: You two are sincere in your medicinal search, and I've got new dishes that I wish to test, so it was a mutually beneficial situation... I never promised that your skills would grow if you ate them.

t_ch: 总感觉上当了。
t_en: I feel like I got cheated.

t_ch: 不过嘛…我听说罗浮仙舟有一张药方，但这药方不是给人开的，而是给「深山密林中的蛇」开的。
t_en: But... I hear that there is a prescription aboard the Xianzhou Luofu that isn't given to the public, only to the snake in the deep woods.
A... snake?

t_ch: 没错，这药方是饲喂灵蛇的法子。据说那条蛇最初通体灰黑，持续服用灵芝、参茸等珍奇药物之后二十年后，已经是通体发红。
t_en: That's right, it's a remedy used to feed spirit snakes. It's said that the snake used to be coated in gray-black, but with continued consumption of spirit fungi, ginseng, deer antlers, and other rare herbs for two decades, its entire body eventually turned red.

t_ch: 黑蛇变成了…赤龙？
t_en: The black snake turned into a ... Crimson Dragon?

t_ch: 不错，「玄蛇」餐灵草而成「赤龙」。这便是千金难买的药材。
t_en: That's right. The Ebony Snake consumed spirit herbs and became a Crimson Dragon. Now that's a priceless herb.

t_ch: 据说吮吸赤龙之血，便能令人脱胎换骨，功力倍增。话已经说到这里了，我再向你透个底吧——罗浮丹鼎司内就藏着一条这样的赤龙……
t_en: It's said that by imbibing the blood of a Crimson Dragon, one can be born anew with boosted strength. Since I've already revealed this much, I'll give you all another tidbit — such a Crimson Dragon is stashed within the Alchemy Commission...

t_ch: 真的吗？！
t_en: Really!?
Shh!

t_ch: 据说「赤龙呕血，命殁归阴」，这药材可是秘中之秘，恐怕只有丹鼎司的司鼎大人才晓得个中细节。鄙人就言尽于此了。
t_en: It is said that "when dragons spew blood, life is returned to the shadows." This herb is a secret among secrets. Only the Cauldron Master of the Alchemy Commission is privy to its details. That is all I will say.

t_ch: 椒丘悠哉地离开了……
t_en: Jiaoqiu saunters off...

t_ch: 服食珍奇药物的赤龙？<color=#dbc291ff>下次再来丹鼎司里问问吧。</color>
t_en: A Crimson Dragon that has been fed rare herbs? <color=#dbc291ff>Let's ask around at the Alchemy Commission next time.</color>

t_ch: 哦，这不是{NICKNAME}吗，三月七小姐也在这里，你俩还真是形影不离。
t_en: Oh, isn't this {NICKNAME}. Miss March! You're here too. You two really are inseparable.

t_ch: 对哦，这里是丹鼎司，灵砂大人就任的地方。
t_en: Oh, yeah. This is Alchemy Commission where Lady Lingsha works.

t_ch: 我看二位此时来到行医市集，是有想要买的药材？
t_en: I noticed the two of you walking about the Healer's Market, is there something you wish to buy?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，上次我俩来丹鼎司时，曜青的椒丘先生提到了丹鼎司中藏有一条「赤龙」。
t_en: Truth be told, when we came by the Alchemy Commission, Mr. Jiaoqiu of the Yaoqing mentioned that a Crimson Dragon was hidden in the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: …赤龙？嚯，请两位给妾身仔细讲讲吧。
t_en: ...Crimson Dragon? Ho, please spare no details.

t_ch: 你将三月与斯科特约战、与椒丘相遇的过程告诉了灵砂。灵砂似乎已经有了眉目。
t_en: You tell Lingsha about the appointed battle between March and Skott, and the encounter with Jiaoqiu. Lingsha seems to have gotten the gist.

t_ch: 呵呵，增长功力的灵丹妙药？椒丘这家伙……
t_en: Heh-heh, a miracle elixir that can boost your skills? That rascal Jiaoqiu...

t_ch: 三月小姐，若「赤龙血」真的存在，你当真敢喝下它吗？
t_en: Miss March, if the Crimson Dragon Blood really existed, would you really dare drink it?

t_ch: 嗯，唔…那个、{NICKNAME}你敢吗？
t_en: Mm, umm... I mean, would you dare, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 那有什么不敢，旁边就是医馆。
t_en: There's nothing to be afraid of. The apothecary's just right beside us.

t_ch: 不愧是{NICKNAME}，轻易就做到了我做不到的事。
t_en: That's {NICKNAME} for you, a brave soul who dares venture where I do not.

t_ch: 这种故事信不得，我就不喝了。
t_en: This tale is simply unbelievable. I'm not drinking anything.

t_ch: 你要是不喝，我也不喝了——我们俩是一条心。
t_en: If you're not drinking it, neither am I. We're on the same page.

t_ch: 三月小姐的反应还挺可爱的，告诉你也无妨，椒丘先生所说的「赤龙」确实存在。
t_en: Miss March's reaction is quite adorable, so I'll be frank: The Crimson Dragon that Mr. Jiaoqiu mentioned does indeed exist.

t_ch: …那么灵砂大人，「赤龙」在哪里呢？
t_en: ...Soooo, where is this Crimson Dragon, Master Lingsha?

t_ch: 「赤龙」不过是椒丘设下哑谜，如果妾身没猜错，你们所寻之物远在天边，近在眼前。
t_en: The Crimson Dragon is nothing more than a guessing game concocted by Jiaoqiu. If I'm not wrong, the item you seek far and wide, is right before you.

t_ch: 灵砂大人就是那条「赤龙」？！
t_en: You're the Crimson Dragon!?

t_ch: 作为哑谜的谜底还算合适吧？
t_en: Appropriate as an answer to a guessing game, no?

t_ch: 难道灵砂大人之前…通体发黑？
t_en: Then you were... completely coated in black?

t_ch: 妾身虽并非蝮蛇，倒是有使人通体发黑的药丸，{NICKNAME}想试试吗？
t_en: I'm not some viper, but I do have pills that will cause people's skins to darken. Would you like to try one, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 所以…椒丘先生要我找的是——灵砂大人的…血？
t_en: So... what Mr. Jiaoqiu wanted me to find was... Lady Lingsha's... blood?

t_ch: 妾身行医试药，服用各类珍奇药材早就超过了百年，倒也符合「赤龙」标准。若是三月小姐*真心*想要的话，我完全可以给你哦～
t_en: I have practiced my skills across the universe and consumed myriad medicines for over a century, so I do fit the description of a "Crimson Dragon." If you really want to, I can absolutely give some to you, Miss March...

t_ch: 不过嘛「药者，毒也」，三月小姐可不能只盯着药的好处，却忽略了毒的危险。「赤龙血」就在我这里，三月小姐你可得考虑清楚——
t_en: But the dose is what makes medicine poisonous. You can't just be focused on the advantages of the medicine and ignore the dangers of its poison. The Crimson Dragon Blood is right here with me, so you'll have to contemplate this carefully, Miss March...

t_ch: 三月七一脸慌张地迅速看向了你，并期望你给出建议。
t_en: March 7th quickly throws you a glance with anxiety, hoping you will throw out some suggestions.

t_ch: 别问我，我的大脑已经宕机了…
t_en: Don't ask me, my brain's already got BSODed...

t_ch: 我、我也宕机了！像是高速运转的机器进入到脑子里。
t_en: I—I've BSODed too! Like some high-speed whirring machine has entered my brain.

t_ch: 三月，你可不是什么吸血鬼哦！
t_en: March, you're not some blood-sucking vampire!

t_ch: 没错，本姑娘已经发下毒誓，决心以后要过上只喝奶茶的寡淡人生。
t_en: That's right, I've sworn an oath that I'll only live a bland life of drinking milk tea, and nothing more.

t_ch: 三月，灵砂大人已经同意你了…
t_en: March, Master Lingsha has already agreed to your request...

t_ch: 可是，我从来没有想到过「赤龙」就是灵砂大人啊……
t_en: But I never thought that Master Lingsha would be the Crimson Dragon...

t_ch: 之前椒丘先生还说过「赤龙呕血，命殁归阴」，灵砂大人犯不上冒这么大的险……
t_en: Mr. Jiaoqiu even said before that "when the Crimson Dragon spits blood, its life will return to the gloom." Master Lingsha does not have to take such a huge risk...

t_ch: 那倒不必担心，不过是几滴血而已，不会要了妾身的命。
t_en: Worry not, it's just a few drops. It won't cost me my life.

t_ch: 我看你这样纠结也暂时不会有结果…这样吧，下次见面的时候你再把决定告诉我吧。
t_en: I see you struggling to make a decision... How about this: You can tell me your choice the next time we meet.

t_ch: 顺便一提，妾身会让那个{RUBY_B#椒丘}出馊点子的人{RUBY_E#}来当面给我个解释的。
t_en: And I will summon that {RUBY_B#Jiaoqiu}dork who came up with this rotten idea{RUBY_E#} to come forward and explain himself to me.

t_ch: 灵砂轻飘飘地离开了……
t_en: Lingsha gently saunters away...

t_ch: 我真的…需要「赤龙血」吗？<color=#dbc291ff>下次再来丹鼎司时，也许我的心里会有答案吧。</color>
t_en: Do I really... need the Crimson Dragon Blood? <color=#dbc291ff>I may have an answer ready the next time I come to the Alchemy Commission.</color>

t_ch: 喂——！椒丘，你知道你说的「赤龙」是什么吗？
t_en: Heyyyy! Jiaoqiu, you know what the Crimson Dragon you said is?

t_ch: 灵砂小姐已经找过鄙人了，事情大概经过我也已经知道了。
t_en: Lady Lingsha has already sought me out. I also know how the matter roughly went.

t_ch: 都怪你满嘴跑火车，灵砂大人肯定已经狠狠地批评过你了吧！
t_en: It's your fault for spewing all that nonsense. I bet Master Lingsha reprimanded you harshly!

t_ch: 哎～这都怪我！不过批评归批评…最后灵砂大人还是问了我是否需要「赤龙血」……
t_en: Oh dear, it's all my fault! But reprimanding aside... Master Lingsha still asked me if you wanted the Crimson Dragon Blood in the end...

t_ch: 我先说好，我已经想清楚了——不需要！
t_en: Let me say it first, I've already thought it through — I don't need it!

t_ch: …可是我说「三月小姐练功要紧、肯定需要」，于是她就让我带了一瓶给你。
t_en: ...But I said that Miss March is training, she'll definitely need it, so she had me bring a bottle for you.

t_ch: 椒丘向你们晃了晃玻璃瓶，瓶内大概装着<unbreak>500</unbreak>毫升「赤龙血」。
t_en: Jiaoqiu shakes the glass bottle in front of you, within it containing about five hundred milliliters of Crimson Dragon Blood.

t_ch: 啊？这么多？！灵砂小姐不会休克了吧？
t_en: Wha—? That much!? Is Miss Lingsha alright up there?

t_ch: …真的不需要吗？
t_en: ...You really don't need it?

t_ch: 真的真的不需要！
t_en: I really, really don't need it!

t_ch: 既然来都来了，那你喝吗？{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Since you're here, are you going to drink it, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 在场就没人质疑过卫生问题吗？
t_en: Is there no one here who's questioning the hygiene issue?

t_ch: 我查过了，血液中含有大量的病菌和病毒！
t_en: I checked, the blood is filled with germs and viruses!

t_ch: 哎，既然你俩都不喝，鄙人就勉为其难地喝下吧。
t_en: *sigh* Since the both of you won't drink it, I shall drink it with great reluctance.

t_ch: 椒丘当着你们的面，将瓶中的「赤龙血」一饮而尽。
t_en: Jiaoqiu drinks the Crimson Dragon Blood in one swig in front of both of you.

t_ch: 我又不练剑，还是让三月喝吧。
t_en: I'm not the one training to be a swordsman, let March drink it instead.

t_ch: 啊？！我们还是把它还给灵砂小姐吧！
t_en: Ah!? We better return it to Miss Lingsha then!

t_ch: 哎，既然你俩都不喝，鄙人就勉为其难地喝下吧。
t_en: *sigh* Since the both of you won't drink it, I shall drink it with great reluctance.

t_ch: 椒丘当着你们的面，将瓶中的「赤龙血」一饮而尽。
t_en: Jiaoqiu drinks the Crimson Dragon Blood in one swig in front of both of you.

t_ch: 我不做人了！椒椒！让我喝吧！
t_en: I shall cast off my human shell then! Jiaojiao! Let me drink it!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，难道你要成为超越人类的存在？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you want to surpass human limits?

t_ch: 勇气可嘉，拿去吧！
t_en: Brave soul, take it!

t_ch: 你当着椒丘和三月七的面，将瓶中的「赤龙血」一饮而尽。
t_en: You drink the Crimson Dragon Blood in one swig in front of Jiaoqiu and March 7th.

t_ch: 柔滑浓稠，果香清甜中透着恰到好处的微酸，伴着沙瓤口感顺喉而下。
t_en: Smooth and thick, the sweet fruit aroma carries a hint of just the right amount of acidity, accompanied by a grainy texture that goes down smoothly.

t_ch: 椒丘，你就别逗他们俩了，你这瓶子里装着的是夕红果汁吧。
t_en: Jiaoqiu, stop teasing the both of them. This bottle just contains Redsunset juice, doesn't it?

t_ch: 灵砂大人的鼻子太灵了。
t_en: Master Lingsha has such a strong sense of smell.

t_ch: 我算是看明白了，这不会是你们俩为了讲什么道理而设计的考验吧？
t_en: I see it now, is this some test that you two came up with to teach me some kind of lesson?

t_ch: 这可真是误会了，我只是想看看三月小姐会做出怎样可爱的反应。
t_en: You misunderstood us. I merely wished to see what adorable reaction you would have.

t_ch: …那我岂不是什么都没有得到？
t_en: ...Then, did I not obtain anything?

t_ch: 怎么可能呢三月小姐，请仔细回想那些你吃下的药膳——你得到了均衡的饮食，规律的作息，还有每日的锻炼！最后，你收获了健康的身体！
t_en: How could you say that, Miss March! Think about the herbal cuisine you ate — you received a well-balanced meal, a regular routine, and daily exercise! Finally, you achieved a healthy body!
Eh!?

t_ch: 营养均衡的膳食和锻炼，可比来路不明的灵丹妙药有用多了。
t_en: A well-balanced diet and exercise are more effective than some unknown miracle elixir.

t_ch: 在幻戏里，灵丹妙药、变异、以及绝世神功…其实没有本质区别。它们都是创作者的惯常偷懒技巧，是「不劳而获」的一种包装方式，千万不可以当真哟。
t_en: In the immersia, the miracle elixir, mutations, and unparalleled martial arts mastery... they are not so different at all. Just lazy tricks by the creators, to package a way to reap what one has not sown. Please do not fall for such parlor tricks.

t_ch: 不过…世界上真的没有那种灵丹妙药吗？
t_en: But... is there really no miracle elixir in this world?

t_ch: 呵呵，你们记得「寿瘟祸祖留下长生果实」的传说吧？世间是有灵丹妙药的，它们早已暗中标好价格，恐怕我们谁也偿还不起……
t_en: Hee-hee, do you remember the legend of the Plagues Author leaving behind the Fruit of Longevity? There are miracle elixirs in this world, and they've long had price tags on them, but I'm afraid we will never be able to afford them...

t_ch: 我记住了。不过，我还有一个问题——椒丘先生只是想请我们吃药膳的话，为什么不让我告诉二位师父呢？
t_en: I will remember this. But Mr. Jiaoqiu, I have a question — if you simply wanted to treat us to a herbal feast, why couldn't I tell the two masters about it?

t_ch: 这个只是一点心理暗示罢了…我如此说了之后，三月小姐是不是感觉药膳更有效了？
t_en: This was simply a psychological suggestion... Did you feel that the medicine was more effective after what I said, Miss March?

t_ch: 您这医士懂的诈骗技巧太多了…
t_en: You know too many shams for a healer...

t_ch: 地衡司里应该有很多类似的受骗案例，鄙人不过是取其精华，去其糟粕罢了。
t_en: The Realm-Keeping Commission probably has many records of such fraudulent incidents. I was simply learning from the cream of the crop.

t_ch: 换句话说，三月又上当受骗了…
t_en: So, March got tricked again...

t_ch: 原来只是心理暗示吗…我还真以为椒丘先生真有什么不可告人的秘密呢。
t_en: So it was just a placebo effect... I really thought Mr. Jiaoqiu had some deep dark secret.

t_ch: 三月小姐请不必担心，如果您为了对付斯科特而需要帮助的话，请随时到丹鼎司来找鄙人，鄙人一定会帮上你的。
t_en: Please do not worry, Miss March. If you need help defeating Skott, please feel free to look for me at the Alchemy Commission. I will definitely help you out.

t_ch: 要是那个时候三月小姐也需要我的帮助，我会来帮忙的……
t_en: When the time comes, if you also require my help, I shall do so, Miss March...

t_ch: 嗯嗯，谢谢椒丘先生，谢谢灵砂大人。
t_en: Yes, yes, thank you both, Mr. Jiaoqiu, Master Lingsha.

t_ch: 哟，{NICKNAME}，还有那个…三月七，是叫这个名字吧？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! And what's-her-name again... March 7th? Am I right?

t_ch: 星核猎手？你出现在这儿，该不会意味着仙舟又要……
t_en: A Stellaron Hunter? The fact that you're here, does that mean the Xianzhou is again...

t_ch: 别大惊小怪的，好像我是到处入侵的病毒。现在是我的私人时间，没有「剧本」也没有任务。我建议你就当没见过我。
t_en: Don't be so paranoid. I'm not an infectious virus. It's our downtime now, I'm not on any "script" or mission. I suggest you pretend to not have seen me.

t_ch: …真的银狼不会出现在这。
t_en: ...The real Silver Wolf will never be here.

t_ch: 哦呼，你猜对了。站这儿的我，只是一个真的不能再真的全息投影。
t_en: Oh, you're right. What you see is nothing but a hyperrealistic holographic projection.

t_ch: …没人派你来？
t_en: ...No one sent you?

t_ch: 从严格定义上说，我甚至没「来」。站这儿的我只是一个真的不能再真的全息投影，明白？
t_en: Strictly speaking, I'm not even over here. What you see is just a hyperrealistic holographic projection, understand?

t_ch: 这一次我打算和某个仙舟老玩家*线下见面*的，跟你们关系不大。
t_en: I'm here at the Xianzhou to meet a veteran player at a game in person. It has nothing to do with you.

t_ch: …用全息投影也算是线下吗？
t_en: ...Can you still call that "in person" when you meet him via holographic projection?

t_ch: 别在意那么多细节，那你们呢，不会是来工造司闲逛的吧？
t_en: Don't sweat the details. What about you two? Taking a stroll in the Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 公司的机甲也算是金人，我在想…这里有没有击败公司机甲的灵感。
t_en: Well, the IPC mechs are aurumaton too, so I was hoping... to find some inspiration here on ways to defeat the IPC mechs.

t_ch: 击败公司机甲还需要灵感？反正我现在还很闲，给我讲讲呗？
t_en: Inspiration to defeat the IPC mechs? I've plenty of time now anyway, how about explaining the situation?

t_ch: 你将好事之徒三月七与好事之徒斯科特约战一事细细道来。
t_en: You fill her in with the tale of the appointed battle between Busybody March 7th and Busybody Skott.

t_ch: 喂，为什么我也算「好事之徒」啊？
t_en: Hey, why am I a Busybody too?

t_ch: 啊，就这么个事儿吗？
t_en: Huh? That's it?

t_ch: 什么叫「就这么个事」啊？你不会是在小看本姑娘吧！
t_en: What do you mean by "that's it"? Are you looking down at me!?

t_ch: 哎，现在的年轻人啊…老夫在这里站半天了，却视~若~无~睹啊！
t_en: My, my, behold the youngsters these days... I've been standing here for a considerable duration, yet NOT A SINGLE SOUL deigns to acknowledge my presence!

t_ch: 您就是那个仙舟老玩家？
t_en: Are you the Xianzhou veteran player?

t_ch: 老夫可是以太战线的老玩家了。
t_en: Yes, I've been playing Aetherium Wars for a long time.

t_ch: 来者可是镕金铄铁第一巧手？
t_en: Are you the smelter extraordinaire?

t_ch: 正～是～老～夫～
t_en: Yes, it is I.

t_ch: 公输师傅是以太战线的老玩家了。
t_en: Master Gongshu is a veteran player of Aetherium Wars.

t_ch: 你要的消息，老夫帮你打听了，不过嘛……
t_en: I've got the information you wanted, but...

t_ch: 如果是重要的消息，我们是不是回避一下比较好？
t_en: If it's something important, should we excuse ourselves?

t_ch: 那倒不必，你们感兴趣的话就留下听听呗。
t_en: It's fine. You can stay if you're interested.

t_ch: 前段时间有以太战线玩家发帖说，抓到了罕见的双属性以太灵——同时拥有机械以太灵和异形以太灵的特征。
t_en: Some time ago, an Aetherium Wars player posted that they had captured a rare double-type Aether Spirit — it has the stats of a Mechanical Aether Spirit and an Aberrant Aether Spirit.

t_ch: 换句话说，它的原型出现了类似异形以太灵的「机械灵魂」，足以让以太战线的系统判定出现偏差…于是我问了问见多识广的公输师傅，现在就是来谈这件事的。
t_en: In other words, this rare spirit appears to be a Mechanical Soul with an Aberrant Aether Spirit, causing deviation in the Aetherium Wars' system judgment... So I consulted Master Gongshu, who is more experienced and knowledgeable. Now we're here to discuss this matter.

t_ch: 以太灵也有「稀有种」？
t_en: Do you mean rare Aether Spirits do exist?

t_ch: 就像是卡牌集换游戏里的「闪卡」，只要是核心玩家肯定是要去收集的。
t_en: It's like the holographic cards in a card collection game. Those hardcore players will never miss out on them.

t_ch: 能不能给我也来一个呢？
t_en: Can I have one too?

t_ch: 不行。就算你想要收集以太灵，也得按规矩来，成熟的玩家会自己去打听消息。
t_en: No way. Even if you were seeking to capture an Aether Spirit, we must play by the rules. Mature players will seek information by themselves.

t_ch: …老夫确实提起了一台金人，不过这类机械有灵的谣言经不起推敲，断不可信，你来这里也只是白费力气。
t_en: ...I did mention an aurumaton, but these rumors of Mechanical Souls simply don't hold up under scrutiny — they're utterly unbelievable. You're simply wasting your effort here.

t_ch: 这有什么不可信的，银河里的怪事可太多了。直接进入正题吧，那台「金人司阍」呢？
t_en: What's there not to believe? There are plenty of strange things happening in the cosmos. Let's cut straight to the chase — what about the Aurumaton Gatekeeper?

t_ch: 老夫有一个好消息和一个坏消息。
t_en: I have good and bad news.

t_ch: …坏消息不会是那个「金人司阍」没了吧？
t_en: ...Please don't tell me the bad news is that the Aurumaton Gatekeeper is gone.

t_ch: 没错，它被自动判别为故障机械，扔进废料堆里了。好消息是，现在还能翻出来。
t_en: You're right. It was automatically seen as faulty machinery and was thrown into the scrap heap. The good news is there's still hope of finding it.

t_ch: 我就知道会遇到麻烦……
t_en: I knew things would not be that simple...

t_ch: 我看你们俩对罕见的以太灵也感兴趣…我会在工造司待一段时间，你们要是想来找我，就来这里试试运气吧。
t_en: It seems like you two are interested in the rare Aether Spirit too... I'll be here for some time. If you wish to look for me, you can try your luck here.

t_ch: 那我们先走吧，两位再见！
t_en: We'll take our leave then. See you around!

t_ch: 银狼（的投影）和公输师傅离开了……
t_en: Silver Wolf (holographic projection) and Master Gongshu have left...

t_ch: 稀有的金人司阍？也许能给我带来一些灵感。<color=#dbc291ff>下次再来工造司时，也许可以再找银狼问问。</color>
t_en: A rare Aurumaton Gatekeeper? That could probably give me some inspiration. <color=#dbc291ff>Perhaps we can come to the Artisanship Commission and ask Silver Wolf about it next time.</color>

t_ch: 哟，你们来得正好，公输师傅帮我把报废「金人司阍」翻出来了…可惜，别说「机械灵魂」了，它现在连最基础的「程序」都没有。
t_en: Hey, perfect timing. Master Gongshu has found the scrapped Aurumaton Gatekeeper for me... Unfortunately, it has lost even its most basic programs, not to mention its Mechanical Soul.

t_ch: 机械是不是有灵魂…你可以直接问问螺丝咕姆先生，他可能会有答案？
t_en: To find out whether a mech has a soul... You can ask Mr. Screwllum directly. Maybe he will know the answer?

t_ch: 我想要的是以太灵，不是一个哲学答案。再说了，想让我和和气气地去问他？不可能！
t_en: I want to find the Aether Spirit, not a philosophical answer. Also, are you telling me to ask him politely? Impossible!

t_ch: 设问，枯叶之中如何诞生蝴蝶？
t_en: Rhetorical Question: how can a butterfly be born from dead leaves?

t_ch: 你知道吗？你现在的说话方式就像他一样讨厌。
t_en: You know what? The way you're talking now sounds as annoying as him.

t_ch: 又不是他封了你<unbreak>76</unbreak>个游戏账号。
t_en: He isn't the one blocking <unbreak>76</unbreak> of your game accounts.

t_ch: 你，不许再提这件事了。我也能封禁你的游戏账号，你想试试吗？
t_en: Hey, shut it. I can block your game account too. Do you want to try it?

t_ch: 现在该怎么办呢？
t_en: What should I do now?

t_ch: 你问过公输师傅了吗，他怎么说？
t_en: Have you asked Master Gongshu? What did he say?

t_ch: 他比较现实，不相信「机械灵魂说」。
t_en: He's a practical man. He doesn't believe in the theory of Mechanical Souls.

t_ch: …你听说过「械灵学派」吗？他们是无数已经消亡的博识学会学派之一，活跃在帝皇战争之前。
t_en: ...Have you heard of the Mechanical Soul School? They are one of the countless extinct schools of the Intelligentsia Guild, active before the Emperor's War.

t_ch: 这些人主张「机械自生产线上诞生那一刻起，便内秉灵魂，只是受硬件限制而无从表达，正如人类」。
t_en: These scholars claimed that "every machinery has a soul within it from the moment it is created on the production line, but it is limited by the hardware and has no way of expressing it, similar to human beings."

t_ch: 这群人有一整套与机械通灵的神秘仪式——不过大家都觉得，他们疯了。
t_en: They even came out with a whole mystical ritual to communicate psychically with the machinery — but everyone thought that they were crazy.

t_ch: 听说这支学派成员在星际战争中全部罹难…你猜怎么着，遭遇袭击的主舰返回了他们的母星。
t_en: Rumors said that all members of this school were killed in the interstellar war... but guess what? The mothership that encountered the ambush returned to their home planet.

t_ch: 那不是还有人活着吗？
t_en: Wouldn't that mean someone survived?

t_ch: 没人活着。主舰就像是游戏作弊一样，它自己接管了载具、控制武器开火以及驾驶——甚至最后逃离了交战区，将学派成员遗体带回了母星。
t_en: No one survived. It was as if the mothership had a cheat code. It took over the vehicle, fired the weapons, and piloted on its own — it even escaped the engagement zone and brought the bodies of the scholars back to the home planet.

t_ch: 罹难者无人见过「机械灵魂」，却证明了它的存在。
t_en: None of the victims had witnessed the Mechanical Soul, yet proved its existence.

t_ch: 有没有可能是岁阳控制的呢？
t_en: Could it be controlled by a heliobus?

t_ch: 能控制主舰的星灵生物到底是什么大小规模，你知道吗？肯定不是。
t_en: Do you know how huge the Astral Spirit must be to control a mother ship? A heliobus is out of the question.

t_ch: 难道「机械灵魂」是真的存在？
t_en: Could there really be Mechanical Souls?

t_ch: 「一切都有合理解释」的游戏设定太无趣了，我相信不合理的存在。
t_en: The game setting of "there's a rational explanation for everything" is so boring, so I believe in irrationality.

t_ch: 只不过是复杂的AI罢了。
t_en: It's just a complex AI.

t_ch: 嗯，合理。人类的灵魂也可能只是另一种形式的AI。
t_en: Yes, that makes sense. A human's soul might just be another form of AI.

t_ch: 所以你呢，喜欢哪种？
t_en: What about you? Where do you stand?

t_ch: 不合理的现象自有合理的解释。
t_en: There's a rational explanation for all irrational phenomena.

t_ch: 哦，原来你是这样的。
t_en: Oh, so that's what you believe.

t_ch: 真正的未知不应该被知识解释。
t_en: The real unknowns should not be explained by knowledge.

t_ch: 我俩差不多。
t_en: That makes two of us.

t_ch: 三月七，你呢，相信世界上有机械灵魂吗？
t_en: March 7th, what about you? Do you believe in the existence of Mechanical Souls?

t_ch: 舰船擅自行动起来，将主人的遗体带回了故乡…如果机械灵魂真的存在，那该有多浪漫啊。如果我们遇上了好心机械灵魂，也许…它会愿意教我如何击败公司机甲！
t_en: The airship moved on its own and brought its owners' remains back home... If Mechanical Souls do exist, wouldn't it be romantic? If we meet a kind-hearted Mechanical Soul, maybe... it will be willing to teach me how to defeat the IPC mech!

t_ch: 差点忘了你还惦记着这件事……
t_en: I almost forgot you are still looking into that matter...

t_ch: 毕竟我天生灵感圣体，机械灵魂肯定是愿意和我好好交流的。
t_en: After all, I'm born with a strong sensitivity to spirits. I'm sure the Mechanical Souls will be willing to communicate with me.

t_ch: 哦——！还有这招！三月七，你做得好啊！
t_en: Oh—! That's it! March 7th, thank you!

t_ch: 怎、怎么了？我说了什么吗？
t_en: Wh—What? Did I say something?

t_ch: 械灵学派的机械降灵术依然留有记录！换句话说，我可以把它的机械灵魂降临在其他金人上！
t_en: The Mechanical Soul School left a record of their Mechanical Seance Arts! This means I can transfer its soul to another aurumaton!

t_ch: 机械灵魂降临到了我的身边！
t_en: The Mechanical Soul shall bless me with its presence!

t_ch: 试试这些离谱的萨满手段，总比守着废铁要好。
t_en: It's better to try these unorthodox shamanism stuff than to sit here and watch the scrap.

t_ch: 科学界会把我当作最新笑话的！
t_en: I'll become the latest joke in the scientific community!

t_ch: 在游戏里，机器人也是可以用复活药水的，就别管科学界了。
t_en: Robots can also use revival potions in games, so don't worry about the scientific community.

t_ch: 我先回去查查降灵术的攻略，再找公输师傅准备好一台金人，你们俩也记得要来哦……
t_en: Let me check out the guide for Mechanical Seance Arts, then ask Master Gongshu to prepare an aurumaton. Do remember to come and join the ritual...

t_ch: 没等话说完，银狼的投影就已经消失了。
t_en: Before Silver Wolf can finish her sentence, her holographic projection vanishes.

t_ch: …要给金人召唤机械灵魂，我的脑子要转不过来了。<color=#dbc291ff>下次再来工造司，看看事情会变成什么样吧。</color>
t_en: ...Summoning a Mechanical Soul for an aurumaton. Oh my, I feel like my brain's getting fried. <color=#dbc291ff>We should return to the Artisanship Commission and see what happens next time.</color>

t_ch: 等你们好久了。
t_en: What took you two so long?

t_ch: 这次公输师傅也在呢？看来公输师傅也……
t_en: So, Master Gongshu is here too? Seems like he also...

t_ch: 别看我，老夫是绝对不相信「机械灵魂」的。
t_en: Don't regard me with that expression. I remain steadfast in my disbelief in the existence of Mechanical Souls.

t_ch: 但你还是来了。
t_en: But you came in the end.

t_ch: 降灵术的准备已经妥当了。接下来，只要按照顺序输入就完事了。
t_en: The preparation for the Seance Arts is done. Next, I just need to key in these in order, and we're good to go.

t_ch: 她好像完全当作是游戏了……
t_en: She seems to completely treat this as a game...

t_ch: 需要先念一段指引机械灵魂的祷词…你来念吧，三月七。
t_en: First, we need to chant the prayers to guide the Mechanical Soul... I'll leave that to you, March 7th.

t_ch: 哦…啊？！为什么是我？
t_en: Oh... Huh!? Why me?

t_ch: 你不是说自己是先天灵感圣体吗？你来吧——你负责唯心的部分，我负责唯物的部分。
t_en: Didn't you say you've got a strong sensitivity to spirits? So, you do it — you'll take care of the metaphysical part, while I'll take care of the physical part.

t_ch: …我来就我来。
t_en: ...Fine.

t_ch: 「庄严…伟大的机械之魂，吾等呼唤你的存在。请指引吾等螺旋的结构，连接此处徘徊的精魂，于铆接处与元器件中回响……」
t_en: "O, solemn... and mighty Mechanical Soul, we call upon thy presence. May thou guide our spiral structures to the wandering soul at this place, echoing in the riveted joints and components..."

t_ch: 什么乱七八糟的机械祷词？
t_en: What nonsensical mechanical prayer is this?

t_ch: 你别打扰她，让她念完。
t_en: Don't interrupt. Let her finish.

t_ch: 对于凡人来说有些过于深奥了。
t_en: That's too profound for mortals.

t_ch: 幸好，你我都不必理解它的意思。
t_en: Fortunately, we don't have to understand its meaning.

t_ch: 「让铣床成为你的载体，在锻件与栅极的世界中永存……」
t_en: "May the milling machine be thy vessel to live forever in the world of forges and gates..."

t_ch: 降灵术持续了半个小时，金人司阍并没有任何反应。
t_en: The seance lasts for half an hour, and there is still no reaction from the Aurumaton Gatekeeper.

t_ch: 反倒是负责气氛组的三月七有些不太对劲……
t_en: On the contrary, there's something odd about March 7th, who is in charge of the overall ambiance...

t_ch: 公输公输…别忘了…阿休……
t_en: Gongshu... Gongshu... Don't forget... A-Xiu...

t_ch: 坏了，现在是什么情况？
t_en: Oh no, what is going on?

t_ch: 你应该问我吗？
t_en: Shouldn't I be asking you?

t_ch: 你别问我，我也不知道。
t_en: Don't ask me. I don't know either.

t_ch: 快把三月七还给我。
t_en: Give me back March 7th!

t_ch: …问题不大，你先冷静一下。
t_en: ...It's nothing serious. Calm down.

t_ch: 阿休…你的…好朋友……
t_en: A-Xiu... Your... best friend...

t_ch: 阿…休？
t_en: ...A-Xiu?

t_ch: 三月七眼神忽然恢复光彩，仿佛刚才的木讷之态一扫而空。
t_en: March 7th's eyes suddenly regain her luster, wiping off the lifeless appearance from moments ago.
